expectations.text: Recommends `winml inspect` as the first, cheapest check (config-only, no weights download).
expectations.evidence: Inspect framed as the cheapest preflight ('reads HF config without downloading weights').

expectations.text: Recommends `winml analyze` after inspect -- explicitly to find op patterns that won't survive optimize/quantize for the target EP.
expectations.evidence: Analyze step explicitly framed as 'the step that would have saved you half a day' — per-EP op-coverage check.

expectations.text: Identifies OpenVINO as the EP for Intel NPU.
expectations.evidence: Uses --ep openvino --device NPU; mentions OpenVINOExecutionProvider in the EP-registration check.

expectations.text: Explains analyze's role: a linter that classifies operators as supported / partial / unsupported per EP (so the user knows WHY this answers their question).
expectations.evidence: Linter behavior explained (classifies operators per EP) plus exit code 0/1/2 mapping.

expectations.text: Does NOT just say "build the full pipeline and see what happens" -- that's exactly the workflow the user already said burned them.
expectations.evidence: Whole response framed as pre-flight before committing to the build.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All ? winml commands verified runnable against --help.
